metadata.title:
TAKE BRIDGE PLANS EAST

MAYOR AND COMMISSIONER
ROY TO HAVE JUNKET TO
CHICAGO IN ENDEAvOR TO
UNRAVEL MIDDLE OVER BE-
GINNING WORK..

pages.raw_text:
Mayor Moore and Commissioner
w. T. Roy are to take all the bridge
plans to Chicago and there submit
them to the Wallace-Coates Engi-
neering Co.


This was decided today at a spe-
cial meeting attended by the
mayor, Commissioners Roy, Ono
and O'Brien, and Councilmen Pratt,
Funk and Gray, the special bridge
committee of the council.


There has been SO much difficulty
about suitable plans and so many
other suggestions and differences
of opinion about the Wallace
Coates plans, that it was thought
advisable to have the plans taken
to Chicago, hoping thus to decide
definitely and begin the already be-
lated work on constructing safe
bridges, something the city now
lacks. The committee will take the
original plans of the Wallace
Coates Co., City Engineer Mcln-
tyres, Engineer Lupfer's, OF the
Traction Co., and the plans OF the
Washington Water Power Co.


While in Chicago the committee
will engage a competent engineer
who will superintend the building
Of the concrete bridges under direc-
ton of the Wallace-Coates Co.


Tonight the mayor will submit
the scheme to the council in COM
mittee Of the whole. IL the sub
committees acts are endorsed by
the council tonight the mayor and
Commissioner Roy will leave for
the east before Saturday.


At this mornings meeting Engi-
neer Lupfer, Of the Traction Co., as
sured the committee that he was
authorized to guarantee that the
company would stand half the ex-
pense of temporary structure
over the river on Washington ST.,
which would be needed during the
course of the erection of the con-
crete bridge. This temporary
structure will be commenced at
once. The probably cost is $2700
The Traction Co. does not agree to
pay its share of this as soon as the
temporary bridge is built, but says
through the engineer, that the city
must deduct this cost from the
amount to be charged against the
company on the permanent struc-
ture. Just what part of this the
company will pay is not yet settled.
